name: APA 7th Edition

general_format.font_note: Font must be consistent throughout the entire paper. Check with publisher/instructor if unsure.
general_format.line_spacing: double-spaced throughout, including title page, abstract, body, references
general_format.paragraph_indent: 0.5 inch for first line of every paragraph

elements: Paper title (centered, bold, title case, upper half of page)
Paper title (centered, bold, title case, upper half of page)
Running head (all caps, flush left, max 50 characters including spaces)
professional_paper.running_head_format: ALL CAPITALS, max 50 characters, no 'Running head:' label

author_note.paragraph_1: ORCID iD with URL for each author (omit authors without ORCID)
author_note.paragraph_2: Any change in affiliation or deaths of authors
author_note.paragraph_3: Disclosures: study registration, open practices, data sharing, conflicts of interest, financial support

title_page.author_name_format: First name, middle initial(s), last name — no titles (Dr.) or degrees (PhD)

abstract.label: Abstract
abstract.label_format: centered, bold, not italicized, no quotation marks
abstract.required_for: scholarly journal articles; optional for student papers unless required by instructor

general_rules: All headings use title case (capitalize important words) in APA 7
All headings are bold in APA 7
Levels are distinguished by italics, indentation, and periods — not font size

level_4.format: Indented, Bold, Title Case, ends with period

level_5.format: Indented, Bold Italic, Title Case, ends with period

numbered_lists.content: full sentences or paragraphs, not phrases
numbered_lists.capitalization: capitalize first word after each number

in_text_citations.signal_phrase_tense: past tense or present perfect (e.g., Jones (1998) found / has found)

three_or_more_authors.note: APA 7 uses et al. from first citation for 3+ authors (changed from APA 6)

ambiguous_et_al.note: If et al. would create ambiguity (same first author, same year), list enough names to differentiate
ambiguous_et_al.example: (Fannon, Chan, Ramirez, et al., 2019) vs (Fannon, Chan, Montego, et al., 2019)

short_under_40_words.citation: (Author, Year, p. X) or (Author, Year, pp. X-Y)

long_40_or_more_words.format: Block quotation — new line, indented 0.5 inch from left margin, no quotation marks
long_40_or_more_words.spacing: double-spaced, no extra blank lines before or after

direct_quotes.no_page_number_sources: Use paragraph number (para. 4), chapter, section, table, or other locator

special_cases.group_author: Spell out in full on first citation; abbreviate if commonly known (e.g., APA)
special_cases.no_author: Use title (italicized for books, in quotes for articles), truncated if long
special_cases.secondary_source: Johnson argued... (as cited in Smith, 2003, p. 102) — only secondary source in reference list
special_cases.personal_communication: (E. Robbins, personal communication, January 4, 2001) — NOT in reference list

general_rules.label: References
general_rules.same_author_same_year: alphabetize by title, add letter suffix (2020a, 2020b)

author_rules.separator: commas between authors, & before last author
author_rules.up_to_20_authors: list all — new in APA 7 (was 6 in APA 6)
author_rules.21_or_more_authors: List first 19, then ellipsis (...), then final author name — no & before last
author_rules.anonymous: Use 'Anonymous' only if work is signed Anonymous

title_rules.books_reports_webpages: Sentence case — capitalize only first word, first word after colon/dash, proper nouns
title_rules.journal_names: Title Case — capitalize all major words

doi_url_rules.label: No 'DOI:' label in APA 7 (removed from APA 6)
doi_url_rules.retrieved_from: Only use 'Retrieved from' when retrieval date is also needed (changeable content)

journal_article.template: Author, A. A., & Author, B. B. (Year). Title of article. Journal Name, volume(issue), page–page. https://doi.org/xxxxx
journal_article.example: Soto, C. J., & John, O. P. (2017). The next big five inventory. Journal of Personality and Social Psychology, 113(1), 117–143.

journal_article_no_doi.template: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of article. Journal Name, volume(issue), page–page. URL

book.template: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of work: Subtitle. Publisher. https://doi.org/xxxxx
book.example: Belcher, W. (2019). Writing your journal article in twelve weeks (2nd ed.). University of Chicago Press.

book_chapter.template: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of chapter. In E. Editor (Ed.), Title of book (pp. XX–XX). Publisher. https://doi.org/xxxxx

edited_book.template: Editor, E. E. (Ed.). (Year). Title of work. Publisher.

translated_book.template: Author, A. A. (Year). Title (T. Translator, Trans.). Publisher. (Original work published YEAR)

report.template: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of report. Publisher/Organization. URL

webpage.template: Author, A. A. (Year, Month Day). Title of page. Site Name. URL
webpage.no_date: Author, A. A. (n.d.). Title of page. Site Name. URL
webpage.changeable_content: Add 'Retrieved Month Day, Year, from URL'

newspaper_article_online.template: Author, A. A. (Year, Month Day). Title of article. Newspaper Name. URL
newspaper_article_online.note: Do not italicize article title; italicize newspaper name

wikipedia.note: Treated as reference work entry, not generic webpage
wikipedia.template: Title of article. (Year, Month Day). In Wikipedia. URL of archived version

footnotes.placement: bottom of page OR endnotes section
footnotes.format: superscript number in text, matching number at bottom of page
footnotes.use_for: supplementary details that would interrupt flow if in body

appendices.label: Appendix A, Appendix B (if multiple)
appendices.use_for: detailed materials, instruments, or data not suitable for body

tables.numbering: Arabic numerals, in order mentioned in text (Table 1, Table 2...)
tables.title_format: italic, title case, left-aligned, on line below number
tables.borders: top and bottom of table, below column headings, above table spanners — no vertical borders
tables.spacing: double for number/title/notes; single, 1.5, or double for body
tables.stub_column: left-aligned entries beneath centered stub heading

notes.order: General note → Specific note → Probability note
notes.general: Note. Explanation of abbreviations, symbols, or overall context
notes.specific: Superscript lowercase letters (a, b, c); first footnote always superscript a
notes.probability: Asterisks for significance levels; consistent use throughout paper

tables.abbreviations: All abbreviations must be explained in notes

figures.numbering: Arabic numerals, in order mentioned in text (Figure 1, Figure 2...)
figures.title_format: italic, title case, left-aligned, on line below number
figures.legend: appears within or below image, words in title case
figures.notes: same order as tables: general, specific, probability
figures.notes_format: double-spaced, left-aligned, same font as paper
figures.permission: written permission required for reuse; credit given in caption

use_words_for: numbers zero through nine (when not before a unit of measurement)
use_numerals_for: numbers before units of measurement (4 km, 5 mg)
numbers representing time, dates, ages, scores
numbers in a series when any is 10 or above

statistics.p_values: report exact values (p = .032) not just p < .05 unless below .001

abbreviations.define_on_first_use: Write out in full first time, then abbreviate: American Psychological Association (APA)

latin_abbreviations.e.g.,: for example — use in parenthetical only
latin_abbreviations.i.e.,: that is — use in parenthetical only

bias_free_language.person_first: prefer 'a man with epilepsy' over 'an epileptic'
bias_free_language.singular_they: use 'they/them' for unknown or nonbinary gender — APA 7 endorsed
bias_free_language.avoid: adjectives used as nouns for groups (e.g., 'the poor' → 'people living in poverty')
bias_free_language.specificity: use specific labels over general ones

verb_tense.literature_review: past tense or present perfect (found, has found)

professional_papers.label: No 'Running head:' label — removed in APA 7
professional_papers.content: identical to paper title or meaningful shortened version

page_numbers.placement: top right of every page including title page

key_changes_from_apa6: et al. used from first citation for 3+ authors (was 6+ in APA 6)
Up to 20 authors listed in references (was 6 in APA 6)
Running head not required for student papers
'Running head:' label removed from professional papers
DOIs formatted as hyperlinks, 'DOI:' label removed
'Retrieved from' only used when retrieval date is included
Level 3–5 headings updated (all now bold, title case)
Separate title page formats for student vs professional papers
Font options expanded beyond Times New Roman
